mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by the Consumer Price Index level remaining elevated, compresses corporate profit margins and increases the discount rate for future earnings, leading to negative equity returns. The signal is active when inflation is above a nominal threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest CPIAUCSL value is 313.534, exceeding the 300 threshold.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition (CPIAUCSL > 300) is met over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates broad economic strength and robust consumer demand, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive forward returns. This is a continuously active, trend-following signal.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows a rolling 36-month correlation below 0.05 with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in retail sales and a rise in the unemployment rate signals a sharp contraction in consumer health, a leading indicator of recession. This joint signal of weakening demand and labor market stress predicts negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The specified joint trigger condition does not precede negative 60-day S&P 500 returns with a hit rate significantly greater than the unconditional probability (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This indicates that firms are successfully meeting demand, leading to earnings growth and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient (IC) between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward returns fails to remain positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a restrictive federal funds rate aggressively tightens financial conditions to curb price pressures. This interaction significantly raises the risk of a hard landing, leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS (5.33) and CPIAUCSL (313.534) meet the trigger conditions.
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions are met, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are not statistically significantly lower than in periods where only one or neither is met.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above a key psychological and cyclical level signals a definitive weakening of the labor market. This triggers fears of recession and reduced consumer spending, causing a short-term negative reaction in equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Latest UNRATE value is 4.3, exceeding the 4.0% threshold.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not negative over a full business cycle backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts is a powerful leading indicator of a broad economic slowdown, as construction has a high multiplier effect. This forward-looking weakness in a key sector negatively impacts investor sentiment and future earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in HOUST has a rolling 36-month correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns that is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank maintains a high policy rate while industrial production is contracting, it signals a potential policy error of overtightening into a slowdown. This combination of restrictive monetary policy and real economy weakness is highly detrimental to corporate profitability and equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 5.33, meeting the rate condition.
failure_condition: The median 20-day S&P 500 return in months where the trigger condition is met is not lower than the overall median return with statistical significance (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05).
